content.body: Combustion is the exothermic reaction of a fuel with oxygen to release heat and light energy.

COMPLETE COMBUSTION: Excess oxygen. Products = CO₂ + H₂O only. Blue flame.
INCOMPLETE COMBUSTION: Limited oxygen. Products = CO (toxic) + carbon (soot) + H₂O. Yellow/orange smoky flame.

Complete combustion of methane: CH₄ + 2O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O
Incomplete combustion: CH₄ + 3/2 O₂ → CO + 2H₂O

FOSSIL FUELS: Coal, crude oil, natural gas — formed from ancient organisms over millions of years. Non-renewable resources.
FRACTIONS OF CRUDE OIL (by fractional distillation): LPG, petrol/gasoline, naphtha, kerosene, diesel, fuel oil, bitumen
ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS: CO₂ → greenhouse effect/global warming. SO₂ → acid rain. CO → poisonous. Particulates (soot) → respiratory problems. NO_x → acid rain and smog.

questions.question: Complete combustion of hydrocarbons produces:
questions.explanation: With excess oxygen: carbon → CO₂, hydrogen → H₂O. Complete combustion.

questions.question: Carbon monoxide (CO) is dangerous because:
options: It combines with haemoglobin preventing O₂ transport
questions.answer: It combines with haemoglobin preventing O₂ transport
questions.explanation: CO binds to haemoglobin 250× stronger than O₂, blocking oxygen delivery to cells.

questions.question: Incomplete combustion occurs when there is:
options: Too much fuel and excess oxygen
questions.explanation: Restricted air supply → incomplete combustion → CO and soot produced instead of CO₂.

questions.question: Fossil fuels are non-renewable because:
options: They formed over millions of years and cannot be quickly replaced
questions.answer: They formed over millions of years and cannot be quickly replaced
questions.explanation: Fossil fuels take millions of years to form — once used, they cannot be replenished on human timescales.

questions.question: Which fraction of crude oil has the lowest boiling point?
questions.explanation: LPG (C3-C4) has very low boiling point — it comes off first at top of fractionating column.

questions.question: Acid rain is mainly caused by:
options: SO₂ and NO_x combining with water in atmosphere
questions.answer: SO₂ and NO_x combining with water in atmosphere
questions.explanation: SO₂ + H₂O → H₂SO₃/H₂SO₄. NO₂ + H₂O → HNO₃. Both form acid rain.

questions.question: The calorific value of a fuel measures:
options: Energy released per unit mass of fuel burned
questions.answer: Energy released per unit mass of fuel burned
questions.explanation: Calorific value (kJ/kg or MJ/kg) is the heat energy released when 1 kg of fuel burns completely.

questions.question: Natural gas is mainly composed of:
questions.explanation: Natural gas is predominantly methane (CH₄), with small amounts of ethane, propane, etc.

questions.question: Which fuel produces the least CO₂ per unit of energy?
questions.explanation: Natural gas (CH₄) has highest H:C ratio — produces more H₂O and less CO₂ per joule of energy.

questions.question: Biofuels are considered carbon-neutral because:
options: CO₂ released when burned was absorbed during plant growth
questions.answer: CO₂ released when burned was absorbed during plant growth
questions.explanation: Plants absorb CO₂ as they grow — burning biofuel releases that same CO₂ back. Net carbon = zero (approximately).

questions.question: Cracking of long-chain hydrocarbons produces:
questions.explanation: Thermal or catalytic cracking breaks long chains → shorter, more useful hydrocarbons + alkenes.

questions.question: Catalytic converters in car exhausts convert CO to:
questions.explanation: Catalytic converter: 2CO + O₂ → 2CO₂. Converts toxic CO to less harmful CO₂.

questions.question: Petrol engines produce NO_x because:
options: High engine temperatures cause N₂ + O₂ → NO
questions.answer: High engine temperatures cause N₂ + O₂ → NO
questions.explanation: At high combustion temperatures, atmospheric N₂ reacts with O₂ to form NO (nitrogen oxide).

questions.question: Coal is mainly composed of:
questions.explanation: Coal is predominantly carbon (plus some H, S, N impurities).

questions.question: Energy from burning fuels is originally from:
questions.explanation: Ancient organisms stored solar energy through photosynthesis. Fossil fuels are this energy stored chemically over millions of years.

questions.question: The greenhouse effect is mainly caused by:
options: CO₂ and CH₄ trapping infrared radiation
questions.answer: CO₂ and CH₄ trapping infrared radiation
questions.explanation: Greenhouse gases (CO₂, CH₄, H₂O vapour) absorb infrared radiation re-emitted from Earth's surface, warming it.

questions.question: Hydrogen fuel cells produce what as the only product?
questions.explanation: H₂ + ½O₂ → H₂O. Hydrogen fuel cells are zero-emission — water is the only product.

questions.question: Which combustion product makes limewater turn milky?
questions.explanation: CO₂ + Ca(OH)₂ → CaCO₃ (white precipitate) + H₂O. This test confirms CO₂ in combustion products.

questions.question: Petrol (octane C₈H₁₈) complete combustion: C₈H₁₈ + ?O₂ → 8CO₂ + 9H₂O. ? =
questions.explanation: Balance: O needed = 8×2 + 9×1 = 25 atoms = 12.5 O₂ molecules.

questions.question: A yellow sooty flame from a Bunsen burner indicates:
questions.explanation: Yellow flame = incomplete combustion (air hole partially closed). Carbon soot makes flame yellow.
